title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 10: Numbers and Patterns
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Lily drew some stars on her page. How many stars did Lily draw?
questions.image_description: Four outlined five-point stars in a row.
questions.solution: Count each star one by one: 1, 2, 3, 4. There are 4 stars.

questions.question_text: What number is missing? 2 + 3 = ?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='45' cy='30' r='8'/><text x='65' y='36' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>+</text><circle cx='90' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='115' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='140' cy='30' r='8'/><text x='160' y='36' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>= ?</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Two circles plus three circles equals a question mark.
questions.solution: Adding 2 and 3 gives 5.

questions.question_text: Which shape in the picture has exactly three sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 250 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><polygon points='80,50 100,10 120,50'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='20'/><polygon points='200,30 220,10 240,30 220,50'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A square, a triangle, a circle, and a rhombus in a row.
questions.solution: A triangle is the shape with exactly three sides.

questions.question_text: What shape comes next in the pattern circle, square, circle, square, ?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 250 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='12'/><rect x='50' y='8' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='100' cy='20' r='12'/><rect x='130' y='8' width='24' height='24'/><text x='180' y='26' font-size='18' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>?</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A repeating pattern of circle, square, circle, square, then a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates circle and square, so after a square comes a circle.

questions.question_text: Which of these numbers is the greatest?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><text x='21' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>3</text><rect x='60' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><text x='71' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>6</text><rect x='110' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><text x='121' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>8</text><rect x='160' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><text x='171' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>5</text><rect x='210' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><text x='221' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>9</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Five boxed numbers in a row: 3, 6, 8, 5, 9.
questions.solution: Among 3, 6, 9, 5, and 8, the largest number is 9.

questions.question_text: How many apples are drawn inside the basket?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><path d='M20 40 Q80 80 140 40 L130 70 Q80 85 30 70 Z'/><circle cx='50' cy='35' r='8'/><circle cx='70' cy='32' r='8'/><circle cx='90' cy='35' r='8'/><circle cx='110' cy='32' r='8'/><circle cx='70' cy='20' r='8'/><circle cx='90' cy='20' r='8'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A basket containing six small circles representing apples.
questions.solution: Count the circles in the basket: there are 6 apples.

questions.question_text: Lily has 7 stickers and gives 2 of them away. How many stickers does she have left?
questions.solution: 7 minus 2 equals 5 stickers remaining.

questions.question_text: How many corners does the polygon below have?
questions.solution: A pentagon has 5 corners (vertices).

questions.question_text: What number continues this sequence: 2, 4, 6, 8, ?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><text x='21' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>2</text><rect x='60' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><text x='71' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>4</text><rect x='110' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><text x='121' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>6</text><rect x='160' y='15' width='35' height='35'/><text x='171' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>8</text><rect x='210' y='15' width='35' height='35' stroke-dasharray='4 2'/><text x='221' y='40' font-size='20' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>?</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A sequence of boxed numbers 2, 4, 6, 8 followed by a dashed box with a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time, so after 8 comes 10.

questions.question_text: How many triangles in total can you see in the figure below?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 110,100 10,100'/><line x1='35' y1='55' x2='85' y2='55'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle with a horizontal line crossing its middle, forming a smaller triangle on top and a trapezoid below.
questions.solution: There is the big outer triangle and the small triangle on top, making 2 triangles in total.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 5 + 4 - 3?
Anna is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Carl. Who is the shortest of the three?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='30' height='60'/><rect x='75' y='35' width='30' height='45'/><rect x='130' y='50' width='30' height='30'/><text x='28' y='15' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>Anna</text><text x='83' y='30' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>Ben</text><text x='138' y='45' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>Carl</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bars of decreasing height labeled Anna, Ben, Carl.
questions.solution: Since Anna > Ben and Ben > Carl, Carl is the shortest.

questions.question_text: Which of the five groups below is made up only of circles?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='15' cy='30' r='10'/><rect x='30' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><text x='10' y='55' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>A</text><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='95' cy='30' r='10'/><text x='75' y='55' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>B</text><polygon points='125,20 140,40 110,40'/><text x='120' y='55' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>C</text><rect x='155' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='180' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><text x='170' y='55' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>D</text><circle cx='220' cy='30' r='10'/><polygon points='240,20 255,40 225,40'/><text x='232' y='55' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>E</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Five options: A has a circle and square, B has two circles, C has a triangle, D has two squares, E has a circle and a triangle.
questions.solution: Option B is the only one made entirely of circles (two circles).

questions.question_text: Which group in the picture contains more objects, the pencils or the erasers?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='6' height='25'/><rect x='20' y='15' width='6' height='25'/><rect x='30' y='15' width='6' height='25'/><rect x='40' y='15' width='6' height='25'/><rect x='50' y='15' width='6' height='25'/><rect x='60' y='15' width='6' height='25'/><text x='20' y='60' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>Pencils</text><rect x='120' y='15' width='15' height='10'/><rect x='140' y='15' width='15' height='10'/><rect x='160' y='15' width='15' height='10'/><rect x='180' y='15' width='15' height='10'/><text x='140' y='60' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>Erasers</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Six thin rectangles labeled Pencils on the left, four small rectangles labeled Erasers on the right.
questions.solution: 6 pencils is more than 4 erasers, so the pencils group has more objects.

questions.question_text: In the sequence 1, 3, 5, 7, ?, what is the next number?
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time (odd numbers), so 7 + 2 = 9.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are in the grid shown below?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 90 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='50' width='20' height='20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A 3 by 3 grid of small squares.
questions.solution: A 3 by 3 grid has 3 times 3 = 9 small squares.

questions.question_text: Three friends share 12 candies equally among themselves. How many candies does each friend receive?
questions.solution: 12 candies divided among 3 friends gives each 4 candies.

questions.question_text: Which shape should replace the question mark at the end of the pattern?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='10'/><polygon points='50,15 65,15 57,35'/><rect x='80' y='15' width='20' height='20'/><circle cx='130' cy='25' r='10'/><polygon points='160,15 175,15 167,35'/><rect x='190' y='15' width='20' height='20'/><circle cx='240' cy='25' r='10'/><text x='232' y='48' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>?</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Repeating pattern: circle, triangle, square, circle, triangle, square, circle, ?
questions.solution: The repeating pattern is circle, triangle, square. After a circle comes a triangle.

questions.question_text: Tom has 4 red balls and twice as many blue balls as red balls. How many balls does he have altogether?
questions.solution: Blue balls: 2 times 4 = 8. Total: 4 + 8 = 12 balls.

questions.question_text: How many small squares make up the big rectangle below?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='25' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='55' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='85' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='100' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='10' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='25' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='40' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='55' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='70' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='85' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='100' y='25' width='15' height='15'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle divided into a 2 by 7 grid of small squares.
questions.solution: The rectangle has 2 rows of 7 squares each: 2 times 7 = 14 squares.

questions.question_text: Maya is older than Jon. Jon is older than Ivy. Ivy is older than Sam. Who is the oldest among these four?
questions.solution: Maya is older than Jon, who is older than Ivy, who is older than Sam. So Maya is the oldest.

questions.question_text: How many edges does the cube drawn in the picture have in total?
questions.solution: A cube has 12 edges: 4 on the top face, 4 on the bottom face, and 4 vertical edges.

questions.question_text: A box holds 10 marbles. After 3 more marbles are put in and then 5 marbles are taken out, how many marbles remain in the box?
questions.solution: Start with 10, add 3 to get 13, take 5 away to get 8 marbles.

questions.question_text: Tiles are numbered 1 to 5 in a row and tile 2 is shaded. If the row is mirrored left-to-right, which numbered position now holds the shaded tile?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20' fill='#d0d0d0'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='90' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><text x='18' y='50' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>1</text><text x='38' y='50' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>2</text><text x='58' y='50' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>3</text><text x='78' y='50' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>4</text><text x='98' y='50' font-size='10' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>5</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Five tiles numbered 1 to 5, with tile 2 shaded.
questions.solution: Mirroring position 2 from the left across the middle gives the 2nd tile from the right, which is position 4.
